titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Nelson Beach, Jr. Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Andrea Wong and Martha Lund Smalley

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 13, 2020 at 23:27

did.abstract: Correspondence, writings, collected material, and biographical records provide valuable documentation of Beach's Yale education, his work with the YMCA in France during World War I, his ministry, his contributions to the Congregational denomination, and the work of the Connecticut Civil Rights Commission. David Nelson Beach, Jr. (1894-1990) was a clergyman in New England and Minnesota, including at First Church of Christ (Center Church) in New Haven from 1943 to 1960. He served on the Connecticut Civil Rights Commission from 1948 to 1960 and played an influential role in the denominational merger that created the United Church of Christ in 1957.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Nelson Beach, Jr. Papers (RG 60A), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

chronitemset.event: Born in Cambridge, MA, son of Lillian Tappan Beach and David Nelson Beach, who was minister of Prospect Street Church, Cambridge, and later President of Bangor Theological Seminary
Graduated from Phillips Academy
B.A., Yale College, Phi Beta Kappa
Study at Bangor Theological Seminary, before and after YMCA service in France; received B.D. from Bangor in 1927
Married Marguerite Mills, who became the mother of their five children
American Expeditionary Force, YMCA service in France
Ordained for chaplaincy; post-war service as YMCA Secretary at Boston University and University of Maine
Student minister, Congregational Church in Millinocket, ME
Minister, First Church, Guilford, CT
Finished seminary work, received B.D., Yale Divinity School
Minister, Park Place Congregational Church, Pawtucket, RI
Minister, Payson Park Church, Belmont, MA
Minister, First Church, Springfield, MA
D.D., American International College
Minister, Plymouth Church, Minneapolis, MN

Minister, First Church of Christ (Center Church), New Haven. While in New Haven he was chaplain of the Revolutionary Second Company Governor's Footguard, first Chairman of the Human Relations Council, President of the Yale Divinity School Alumni Council
Member of the Connecticut Civil Rights Commission (formerly know as Inter-Racial Commission); Chairman of Commission 1954-1955
Chairman of General Council Commission on Interchurch Relations and Christian Unity
Moderator of General Association of Connecticut and the New Haven Association
Minister, West Dennis Community Church, Cape Cod
Part-time pastorate at Short Beach Community Church, Branford, CT
Died

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These papers continue the record of the Beach Family begun in Record Group 60, which primarily documents the life and work of David Nelson Beach, Jr.'s father, David Nelson Beach, and his uncle, Harlan P. Beach. Descendant of established New England families, graduate of Yale College and Yale Divinity School, David Nelson Beach, Jr. was a respected and influential clergyman for more than half a century, and a systematic "keeper" of records. Besides providing biographical information, Beach's papers include valuable, thorough documentation of two movements in which he was a key player: the movement toward merger of the Congregational Christian Churches and the Evangelical and Reformed Church to form the United Church of Christ, and the Connecticut Civil Rights Commission.

The Family Correspondence of Series I all dates from before 1928 and is primarily between Beach and his fiancé/wife Marguerite Mills Beach or Beach and his parents and siblings. Extensive correspondence from the 1915 to 1918 time period documents Beach's life as a student at Yale and Bangor Theological Seminary, and his service with the YMCA in France during World War I.

The General Correspondence of Series II is not extensive, but provides an overview of Beach's activities in the churches and organizations with which he was involved, including First Church (Center Church) in New Haven. Much of the substantial correspondence of the record group is contained in Series IV and V with the records documenting the Congregational merger and the Connecticut Civil Rights Commission.

The notes in Series III date primarily from Beach's years at Yale College, providing a thorough record of his undergraduate coursework. Beach's writings include sermons and articles related to the nature of Congregationalism. Other notes, drafts, and writings by Beach are contained in Series IV and V.

Series IV contains a variety of records related to the merger that formed the United Church of Christ in 1957, including legal briefs, letters, memos, notes, and material documenting the role of Beach and Center Church, New Haven. These records of a key player who was in favor of the merger provide an important complement to similar records in Record Group No. 52, the Papers of Henry David Gray, an influential opponent of the merger.

Beach's twelve years of service on what was first called the Connecticut Inter-Racial Commission and then the Connecticut Civil Rights Commission came at a crucial period in the development and enforcement of civil rights in the United States. The thorough Commission records retained by Beach include minutes, letters, memos, cases of appeal, conference reports, and other documentation providing a vivid picture of the civil rights climate in Connecticut and surrounding states from 1948 to 1960.

Series VI, Biographical Documentation, includes a variety of materials shedding light on Beach as an individual, his family, and the organizations with which he was involved during his long and active career.

list.item: IV. Records Related to Merger forming UCC, 1947-1981, undated
V. Records Related to Civil Rights, 1948-1981, undated

National Association of Congregational Christian Churches (U.S.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional correspondence is contained in Series III, IV, and V

scopecontent.p: Including re: move to accept pastorate at Center Church, New Haven

did.unittitle: Introduction to the Greek New Testament
New Testament Theology (Prof. Frank Chamberlin Porter)
Old Testament Literature (Prof. C. G. Cumming, Bangor)
Psychology of Childhood (Prof. E. Hershey Sneath)
Psychology of Christian Experience, Psychology of Religion
China as a Mission Field (Prof. Harlan P. Beach)
Miscellaneous class papers and short writings
Writings of David Nelson Beach, Jr.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See Box 15 and Box 16 for various drafts and notes by David Nelson Beach, Jr., related to Congregationalism
See Box 19 for writings of David Nelson Beach, Jr., related to Civil Rights

did.unittitle: Thesis: "Certain Conditions and Tendencies in Practical Theology as Taught Today"

Sermons and talks; "Farewell Sermons preached in Plymouth Church"; "Let Congregationalism March Forward in 1960"; "The Historic Continuities of Conciliar Congregationalism, 1648-1961"; "A Sure Heritage"
"Freedom for Responsibilitiy in American Congregationalism"; "A Husband's Tribute to his Wife"; autobiographical writings; miscellenous sermon notes
"Words of Might" - three months' cycle of Scripture passages; "In My Own Right" - an introduction to personal devotions
Records Related to Merger forming the United Church of Christ (UCC)
Proposals for merger of The Congregational Christian Churches and The Evangelical and Reformed Church
Defendant's trial brief: Cadman Memorial Congregational Society against Helen Kenyon, Moderator of the General Council of the CCC
Documents, memos, correspondence, recommendation of New Haven Association, Constitution of UCC
Review Committees reports, David Nelson Beach, Jr., report to Board of Deacons of Center Church, New Haven, Center Church Amendment
"How Church Union Came" by Malcolm K. Burton, article re: Mayflower Congregation Church lawsuit
Material re: Congregationalism, including memo of John T. Beach re. misuse of term "congregation", notes of David Nelson Beach, Jr.
Material re: Congregationalism, merger; notes and drafts of David Nelson Beach, Jr.
Conference of Commissions of CT, MA, NJ, NY
Eastern States Conference of Commissions Against Discrimination
Documents, memos, correspondence; Education Committee, Inter-Group Relations Committee
Draft of "Training Negroes in the Skilled Trades in Connecticut"
Documents, memos, correspondence, including interview with David Nelson Beach, Jr.
Report: "Comparative Study of Negro and White Dropouts in Selected Connecticut High Schools" by Henry G. Stetler

David Nelson Beach, Jr. writings: "The Federal Civil Rights Commission and Compliance by Conciliation", "The Right of Petition and the Programs of State Public Agencies Involved in the Field of Discrimination"
David Nelson Beach, Jr. draft writings on civil rights
David Nelson Beach, Jr. petition to President Reagan re: right to petition of Iran hostages
David Nelson Beach, Jr. biographical material
In France with YMCA and writings about it
In Guilford, at Yale Divinity School
Personal record books - car expenses, shorthand dictionary
In Springfield, MA , including controversy with Gov. James Curley
In Connecticut; including autobiographical sketch, celebration of 60th anniversary of ordination; genealogical information
Photographs: David Nelson Beach, Jr. and family
Re: John T. Beach and other family members
Miscellaneous programs, worship materials, notes, poems, clippings
Program material from churches served by David Nelson Beach, Jr.
